equity.label: S&P 500 equity pressure
equity.note: Bigger equity drawdowns imply more stress.

rates.label: 2Y Treasury rate pressure
rates.note: Rates pressure reflects both the current 2Y level and any fresh 5-session rise.

inflation.label: Inflation expectations pressure
inflation.note: Inflation pressure reflects both the breakeven level and any fresh 5-session rise.

volatility.label: VIX volatility pressure
volatility.note: Higher implied volatility usually means greater market stress.

title: 2026-02-02 TACO Pressure Index Update: Low at 18.98
description: Today’s retreat-pressure snapshot for 2026-02-02: the TACO Pressure Index closed at 18.98/100, signaling a low pressure regime as inflation expectations and front-end rate pressure shaped the daily read. Explore the score history, daily post, and the four-factor dashboard.
hero_summary: The index is designed to approximate how market stress can build pressure for a softer policy posture. On 2026-02-02, the score printed 18.98/100 in the low regime, with inflation expectations and front-end rate pressure doing most of the work.
